title: CronNet-Holo Series: Experimental Protocols for DEQ Formation, Filamentation, and Quantum Phase Resonance
author: Morató de Dalmases, Luis
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
1-The generation of Dark Energy Quasiparticles (DEQ) 
  File:  1Experimental Protocol for DEQ Generation.pdf   
  Author : Luis Morató de Dalmases 
  Abstract : The generation of Dark Energy Quasiparticles (DEQ) within a controlled plasma environment, and their potential condensation into structured matter.  
  License : Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 (CC-BY 4.0)  (Attribution - NonCommercial - ShareAlike  
  Keywords : DarkEnergy #TimeTheory #DEQ #QuantumPlasma #ChronaCompute #ScientificInnovation #MatterFromVacuum #LinkedInScience 
 2-Experimental Protocol for Phase-Shifted Photons in Quantum Computing 
  File:    2Experimental Protocol for Phase-Shifted Photons.pdf   
  Author : Luis Morató de Dalmases 
  Abstract : Through our most recent protocol—presented in  DEQ6 —we propose a method to extract  quantum information from the phase-dislocated photons  that mimic the structure of dark matter, guided by the resonance dynamics of the  Time Theory . 
  License : Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 (CC-BY 4.0)  (Attribution - NonCommercial - ShareAlike  
  Keywords : DarkEnergy #TimeTheory #DEQ #QuantumPlasma #ChronaCompute #ScientificInnovation #MatterFromVacuum #LinkedInScience 
 3-Experimental Protocol for Dark Matter Formation (DEQ Filamentation Protocol) 
  File: 3 Experimental Protocol for Dark Matter Formation.pdf   
  Author : Luis Morató de Dalmases 
  Abstract : This protocol outlines a  controlled experimental simulation  of dark matter formation, based on the  filamentation and condensation of Dark Energy Quadraparticles (DEQ)  under chronotemporal resonance, as formalized in the DEMF equation. 
  License : Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 (CC-BY 4.0)  (Attribution - NonCommercial - ShareAlike  
  Keywords : DarkEnergy #TimeTheory #DEQ #QuantumPlasma #ChronaCompute #ScientificInnovation #MatterFromVacuum #LinkedInScience
